page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes.

Benchmark data highlights strong single-threaded performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression (35.04 GB/sec), single-threaded bzip3 decompression (14.86 GB/sec), and Geekbench single-core testing (2317.0). Multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall into the average tier, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 8819.0 and uncached memory read speeds of 70.65 GB/sec. The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, which assist in cryptographic and vector calculations.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highcpu-8 provides a high-compute, low-memory profile that minimizes memory overhead costs for CPU-bound tasks. However, the lack of bundled local storage and the low RAM-to-vCPU ratio represent clear tradeoffs for memory-heavy or high-I/O database applications. This server is best suited for compute-bound workloads such as web serving, single-threaded data processing, lightweight software compilation, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it relies entirely on network-attached storage. The hardware profile delivers a low RAM-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, optimizing cost efficiency for CPU-bound tasks by eliminating excess memory overhead. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core capabilities, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2317.0 and high single-threaded compression throughput, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. Supported instruction sets include AVX-512 and Intel AMX for accelerated vector and matrix operations. This instance is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads such as web serving, text processing, and lightweight software compilation where memory requirements are modest.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, delivering top-tier single-core performance for CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-8 is an 8-vCPU compute-optimized instance with 16 GB RAM, featuring top-tier single-threaded performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine delivering top-tier single-core performance and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for CPU-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16 GB RAM.
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and single-threaded compression.
Features Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for vector and matrix acceleration.
No bundled local storage or GPU, requiring network-attached storage.
Optimized for compute-bound tasks, web serving, and text processing.
